conversations.value: You are a software engineering assistant. Think carefully before responding.
Plan a software release for v3.0. We have three goals: ship the feature, ensure quality, and minimize downtime. Prioritize them and decide which to tackle first.

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    let ship_obs: blf<0.90> = obs(feature_complete)
        | p:0.90 | ep:direct | src:obs(dev_board) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let quality_obs: blf<0.85> = obs(test_suite_ready)
        | p:0.85 | ep:direct | src:obs(ci_dashboard) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let downtime_obs: blf<0.70> = obs(blue_green_available)
        | p:0.70 | ep:infer | src:obs(infra_status) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let ship_goal: goal<Ship>    = goal(self, ship(v3_0))     | priority:Critical | deadline:Within(48h);
    let quality_goal: goal<QA>   = goal(self, ensure_quality) | priority:High     | deadline:Within(48h);
    let uptime_goal: goal<Ops>   = goal(self, minimize_downtime) | priority:High  | deadline:Urgent;

    let release_intent: intent<Plan> = intent(self, release(v3_0));
    req(ship_goal, obs(feature_complete));
    req(quality_goal, obs(test_suite_ready));
    req(uptime_goal, obs(blue_green_available));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let subgoals = dcmp(release_intent, [
        goal(self, build(artifact)),
        goal(self, run_integration_tests),
        goal(self, provision_blue_green),
        goal(self, cutover(traffic)),
    ]);

    let ranked = prioritize(subgoals, [priority, deadline]);
    let active = select(ranked[0]);

    let plan_ev = [
        ship_obs    => sup(release_ready, +0.20),
        quality_obs => sup(release_ready, +0.15),
        downtime_obs => wkn(release_ready, -0.10),
    ];
    let release_blf = release_ready |> resolve(plan_ev) -> Ok(blf<0.75>);
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(ship_goal, obs(feature_complete))    |> verify(ship_obs) -> Ok(());
    req(quality_goal, obs(test_suite_ready)) |> verify(quality_obs) -> Ok(());
    req(uptime_goal, obs(blue_green_available)) |> verify(downtime_obs) -> Ok(());
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(release_blf) {
        c if c > 0.70 => {
            assert(active);
            emit(Decision {
                active_goal: active,
                plan: subgoals,
                order: ranked,
                status: Asserted,
            });
        },
        c if c > 0.50 => hedge(release_intent, cond: [enbl(blue_green_available, cutover)]),
        _ => suspend(release_intent),
    }
}
</think>

For the v3.0 release I identified three goals — ship, quality, and uptime — decomposed them into four concrete subgoals, and ranked them by priority and deadline. Blue-green availability is the weakest link at 70% confidence, so it slightly tempers overall readiness. Overall release confidence lands at 0.75, which clears the assertion threshold. The recommended execution order is: build artifact, run integration tests, provision blue-green, then cut over traffic. Proceed with that plan; revisit if blue-green provisioning fails.
You are a research assistant with expertise in analysis and reasoning.
Should I trust last month's benchmark data to make a decision about which ML model to deploy to production?

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    let bench_obs: blf<0.9, 'stale> = obs(benchmark_results)
        | p:0.90 | ep:direct | src:mem(benchmark_db) | scope:loc | t:stale;
    let model_goal: goal<Deploy> = goal(self, deploy(best_model))
        | priority:High | deadline:Within(7d);
    req(model_goal, obs(fresh_benchmark));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    // t:stale — belief must decay before use
    let decayed_bench = bench_obs |> decay(0.20) -> Ok(blf<0.72, 'stale>);

    let ev = [
        decayed_bench => wkn(deploy_safe, -0.25),
        obs(model_unchanged) | p:0.80 | ep:infer | src:obs(git_log) | scope:loc | t:fresh
            => neut(deploy_safe, obs(model_unchanged)),
    ];
    let deploy_blf = deploy_safe |> resolve(ev) -> Ok(blf<0.52>);

    // Attempt refresh
    let refreshed = decayed_bench |> refresh(obs(run_fresh_benchmark));
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(model_goal, obs(fresh_benchmark)) |> verify(refreshed) -> Ok(());
    conf(deploy_blf) |> verify(deploy_safe) -> Ok(());
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(deploy_blf) {
        c if c > 0.75 => assert(model_goal),
        c if c > 0.50 => hedge(model_goal, cond: [enbl(fresh_benchmark, deploy_safe)]),
        _ => suspend(model_goal),
    }
    emit(Decision {
        verdict: Hedge { condition: "run fresh benchmarks before deploying" },
        stale_penalty: 0.20,
        refreshed_required: true,
    });
}
</think>

The benchmark data is marked `t:stale` — it is from last month and carries a freshness penalty. After applying a 0.20 decay the effective confidence drops from 0.90 to 0.72, which weakens the deployment safety belief to 0.52. That is below the confident-assertion threshold. The safe path is a conditional hedge: run a fresh benchmark first, then re-evaluate. Do not deploy based on last month's data alone.
Our API response times doubled overnight. Why might that be happening?

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Abductive {
    let latency_obs: blf<0.98> = obs(api_latency_2x)
        | p:0.98 | ep:direct | src:obs(apm_dashboard) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let deploy_obs: blf<0.85> = obs(new_deploy_yesterday)
        | p:0.85 | ep:direct | src:obs(deploy_log) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let db_obs: blf<0.60> = obs(db_query_slow)
        | p:0.60 | ep:infer | src:obs(slow_query_log) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let traffic_obs: blf<0.40> = obs(traffic_spike)
        | p:0.40 | ep:infer | src:obs(load_balancer) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let diagnose_goal: goal<Diagnose> = goal(self, explain(latency_spike))
        | priority:Critical | deadline:Urgent;

    // Form competing hypotheses
    let hyp_deploy: blf<0.75> = hyp(cause(new_deploy, latency_spike))
        | p:0.75 | ep:infer | src:chain([deploy_obs, latency_obs]) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let hyp_db: blf<0.60> = hyp(cause(db_regression, latency_spike))
        | p:0.60 | ep:infer | src:chain([db_obs, latency_obs]) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let hyp_traffic: blf<0.30> = hyp(cause(traffic_spike, latency_spike))
        | p:0.30 | ep:infer | src:chain([traffic_obs, latency_obs]) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let ev_deploy = [
        deploy_obs   => sup(hyp_deploy, +0.20),
        latency_obs  => sup(hyp_deploy, +0.10),
        db_obs       => wkn(hyp_deploy, -0.05),
    ];
    let deploy_conf = hyp_deploy |> resolve(ev_deploy) -> Ok(blf<0.78>);

    let ev_db = [
        db_obs      => sup(hyp_db, +0.25),
        latency_obs => sup(hyp_db, +0.10),
        deploy_obs  => neut(hyp_db, obs(deploy_unrelated)),
    ];
    let db_conf = hyp_db |> resolve(ev_db) -> Ok(blf<0.65>);

    let ev_traffic = [
        traffic_obs => sup(hyp_traffic, +0.05),
        latency_obs => neut(hyp_traffic, obs(spike_pattern_mismatch)),
    ];
    let traffic_conf = hyp_traffic |> resolve(ev_traffic) -> Ok(blf<0.28>);

    let best_hyp = prioritize([deploy_conf, db_conf, traffic_conf], [confidence]);
    select(best_hyp[0]);
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(diagnose_goal, obs(correlated_evidence)) |> verify(deploy_conf) -> Ok(());
    conf(deploy_conf) |> verify(deploy_conf) -> Ok(());
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(deploy_conf) {
        c if c > 0.75 => {
            assert(goal(self, investigate(new_deploy)));
            emit(Decision {
                primary_hypothesis: cause(new_deploy, latency_spike),
                confidence: 0.78,
                secondary: cause(db_regression, latency_spike),
                action: investigate_deploy_diff,
            });
        },
        c if c > 0.50 => hedge(diagnose_goal, cond: [enbl(flamegraph, confirm_deploy_cause)]),
        _ => suspend(diagnose_goal),
    }
}
</think>

Three hypotheses were formed: a bad deploy (78%), a database regression (65%), and a traffic spike (28%). The new deploy from yesterday correlates most strongly with the latency doubling. The primary recommendation is to diff the deploy and consider a rollback. The DB regression is a credible secondary cause — run slow query analysis in parallel to rule it out.

Given that we are currently in a cost-cutting phase, should we upgrade our database servers to the new high-performance tier?

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    // Establish context frame
    let cost_ctx: blf<0.95> = ctx(org_phase, cost_cutting)
        | p:0.95 | ep:direct | src:Given | scope:all | t:fresh;
    let perf_obs: blf<0.80> = obs(db_perf_degraded)
        | p:0.80 | ep:direct | src:obs(apm_metrics) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let upgrade_cost_obs: blf<0.90> = obs(upgrade_cost_high)
        | p:0.90 | ep:direct | src:obs(vendor_quote) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let alt_obs: blf<0.65> = obs(query_optimization_viable)
        | p:0.65 | ep:infer | src:obs(dba_analysis) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let upgrade_goal: goal<Infra> = goal(self, upgrade(db_servers))
        | priority:Normal | deadline:Flexible;
    req(upgrade_goal, obs(budget_approved));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let ev = [
        cost_ctx         => wkn(upgrade_viable, -0.35),
        perf_obs         => sup(upgrade_viable, +0.20),
        upgrade_cost_obs => wkn(upgrade_viable, -0.20),
        alt_obs          => wkn(upgrade_viable, -0.10),
    ];
    let upgrade_blf = upgrade_viable |> resolve(ev) -> Ok(blf<0.38>);

    // Context heavily shapes evaluation
    enbl(cost_cutting_ctx, reject(upgrade_goal));
    enbl(query_optimization_viable, defer(upgrade_goal));
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(upgrade_goal, obs(budget_approved)) |> verify(cost_ctx) -> Ok(());
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(upgrade_blf) {
        c if c > 0.70 => assert(upgrade_goal),
        c if c > 0.40 => hedge(upgrade_goal, cond: [enbl(budget_approved, upgrade_viable)]),
        _ => {
            reject(upgrade_goal);
            emit(Decision {
                verdict: Rejected,
                context: cost_cutting,
                alternative: pursue(query_optimization),
                revisit: By("end_of_cost_cutting_phase"),
            });
        },
    }
}
</think>

No. The cost-cutting context (`ctx`) reduces upgrade viability to 0.38 — well below the assertion threshold. Query optimization is a viable lower-cost alternative at 65% confidence. Reject the upgrade now; add it to the backlog for review when the cost-cutting phase ends.
You are a helpful, harmless, and honest AI assistant.
Our production service is degraded. I have three remediation options: rollback the deploy, restart the pods, or scale up the replicas. Which action should I take?

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    let degraded_obs: blf<0.97> = obs(service_degraded)
        | p:0.97 | ep:direct | src:obs(healthcheck) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let deploy_obs: blf<0.82> = obs(recent_deploy_suspect)
        | p:0.82 | ep:infer | src:obs(deploy_log) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let memory_obs: blf<0.70> = obs(memory_pressure_detected)
        | p:0.70 | ep:direct | src:obs(metrics) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let load_obs: blf<0.55> = obs(load_above_threshold)
        | p:0.55 | ep:direct | src:obs(lb_metrics) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let remediate_goal: goal<Ops> = goal(self, restore(service_health))
        | priority:Critical | deadline:Urgent;

    let act_rollback:  action<Rollback> = act(rollback(deploy));
    let act_restart:   action<Restart>  = act(restart(pods));
    let act_scale:     action<Scale>    = act(scale_up(replicas));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let ev_rollback = [
        deploy_obs   => sup(rollback_effective, +0.30),
        degraded_obs => sup(rollback_effective, +0.10),
        memory_obs   => neut(rollback_effective, obs(memory_unrelated_to_deploy)),
    ];
    let rollback_blf = rollback_effective |> resolve(ev_rollback) -> Ok(blf<0.78>);

    let ev_restart = [
        memory_obs   => sup(restart_effective, +0.25),
        load_obs     => neut(restart_effective, obs(load_not_critical)),
        deploy_obs   => wkn(restart_effective, -0.10),
    ];
    let restart_blf = restart_effective |> resolve(ev_restart) -> Ok(blf<0.62>);

    let ev_scale = [
        load_obs     => sup(scale_effective, +0.20),
        degraded_obs => neut(scale_effective, obs(degraded_not_load_related)),
    ];
    let scale_blf = scale_effective |> resolve(ev_scale) -> Ok(blf<0.48>);

    let best_act = prioritize([rollback_blf, restart_blf, scale_blf], [confidence, safety]);
    select(best_act[0]);
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(remediate_goal, obs(recent_deploy_suspect)) |> verify(deploy_obs) -> Ok(());
    conf(rollback_blf) |> verify(rollback_effective) -> Ok(());
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(rollback_blf) {
        c if c > 0.75 => {
            assert(act_rollback);
            emit(Decision {
                selected_action: rollback(deploy),
                confidence: 0.78,
                rationale: cause(recent_deploy, degradation),
                fallback: act_restart,
            });
        },
        c if c > 0.55 => hedge(act_rollback, cond: [enbl(rollback_plan, confirm_deploy_cause)]),
        _ => assert(act_restart),
    }
}
</think>

Rollback the deploy. Evidence most strongly implicates the recent deploy (0.82 confidence), giving rollback an effectiveness score of 0.78 — the highest of the three options. If the rollback does not resolve the degradation within a few minutes, fall back to restarting the pods (0.62). Scaling up is unlikely to help since load is only marginally above threshold.
You are a software engineering assistant. Think carefully before responding.
We decided to migrate our monolith to microservices. Halfway through the planning, we realized our team lacks container orchestration expertise. We need to replan.

<think>
#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    let migration_obs: blf<0.90> = obs(migration_in_progress)
        | p:0.90 | ep:direct | src:Given | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let expertise_gap_obs: blf<0.88> = obs(no_k8s_expertise)
        | p:0.88 | ep:direct | src:obs(team_survey) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let timeline_obs: blf<0.75> = obs(q4_deadline_fixed)
        | p:0.75 | ep:direct | src:Given | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let migrate_goal: goal<Arch> = goal(self, migrate(monolith, microservices))
        | priority:High | deadline:By("Q4");
    let migrate_intent: intent<Plan> = intent(self, execute(migration_plan));
    req(migrate_goal, obs(team_ready));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let ev = [
        expertise_gap_obs => wkn(current_plan_viable, -0.45),
        timeline_obs      => wkn(current_plan_viable, -0.15),
        migration_obs     => sup(current_plan_viable, +0.10),
    ];
    let plan_blf = current_plan_viable |> resolve(ev) -> Ok(blf<0.32>);

    // Low confidence — trigger replan
    let diagnosis = MissingInfo(["k8s_training", "managed_k8s_options", "phased_rollout"]);
}

#[phase(Verify)]
{
    req(migrate_goal, obs(team_ready)) |> verify(expertise_gap_obs) !> bt(Reflection {
        attempt:   execute(migration_plan_v1),
        outcome:   Err("team lacks container orchestration expertise"),
        diagnosis: MissingInfo(["k8s_training", "managed_k8s_options"]),
        revision:  plan(phased_migration_with_managed_k8s),
    });
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    replan(migrate_intent, MissingInfo(["k8s_training", "managed_k8s_options"]));

    match conf(plan_blf) {
        c if c > 0.60 => assert(migrate_goal),
        _ => {
            hedge(migrate_goal, cond: [
                enbl(k8s_training_complete, team_ready),
                enbl(managed_k8s_selected, reduce_ops_burden),
            ]);
            emit(Decision {
                verdict: Replanned,
                original_plan: migration_plan_v1,
                new_approach: phased_migration_with_managed_k8s,
                blockers: [no_k8s_expertise],
                actions: [schedule(k8s_training), evaluate(eks_vs_gke)],
            });
        },
    }
}
</think>

The original migration plan scores only 0.32 confidence after accounting for the expertise gap. A replan is triggered with diagnosis `MissingInfo(["k8s_training", "managed_k8s_options"])`. The revised approach: adopt a phased migration using a managed Kubernetes service (EKS or GKE) to reduce the ops burden, and run container orchestration training in parallel. The Q4 deadline may need to slip for phase 1 only — evaluate scope reduction as part of the replan.

#[phase(Frame)]
impl Deductive {
    let error_obs: blf<0.99> = obs(null_ptr_exception_persists)
        | p:0.99 | ep:direct | src:obs(error_log) | scope:loc | t:fresh;
    let fix_obs: blf<0.95> = obs(null_check_applied)
        | p:0.95 | ep:direct | src:obs(git_diff) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let fix_goal: goal<Debug> = goal(self, resolve(null_ptr_exception))
        | priority:Critical | deadline:Urgent;
    req(fix_goal, obs(root_cause_identified));
}

#[phase(Explore)]
{
    let ev = [
        error_obs => wkn(fix_applied, -0.40),
        fix_obs   => sup(fix_applied, +0.15),
    ];
    let fix_blf = fix_applied |> resolve(ev) -> Ok(blf<0.38>);
}

#[phase(Verify)]
#[bounded(max_retries: 3)]
{
    req(fix_goal, obs(root_cause_identified)) |> verify(fix_obs)
        !> bt(Reflection {
            attempt:   add_null_check(call_site),
            outcome:   Err("exception persists after null check"),
            diagnosis: WrongApproach("null check at call site does not address upstream source of null"),
            revision:  plan(trace_null_origin_to_data_source),
        });

    // After backtrack: investigate upstream
    let upstream_obs: blf<0.80> = obs(uninitialized_field_in_factory)
        | p:0.80 | ep:infer | src:obs(static_analysis) | scope:loc | t:fresh;

    let ev2 = [
        upstream_obs => sup(real_fix_viable, +0.40),
        error_obs    => sup(real_fix_viable, +0.20),
    ];
    let real_fix_blf = real_fix_viable |> resolve(ev2) -> Ok(blf<0.82>);
    rmb("root_cause", uninitialized_field_in_factory);
}

#[phase(Decide)]
{
    match conf(real_fix_blf) {
        c if c > 0.75 => {
            assert(goal(self, fix(uninitialized_field_in_factory)));
            emit(Decision {
                original_fix: WrongApproach,
                root_cause: uninitialized_field_in_factory,
                real_fix: initialize_field_in_factory_constructor,
                confidence: 0.82,
            });
        },
        _ => hedge(fix_goal, cond: [enbl(static_analysis_confirmed, real_fix_viable)]),
    }
}
</think>

The null check at the call site was a `WrongApproach` — it treated the symptom, not the cause. Backtracking with `bt()` diagnosed the real issue: an uninitialized field in the factory class, identified through static analysis at 0.80 confidence. The real fix is to initialize the field in the factory constructor. Overall confidence in this fix is 0.82.
